name: Chocolate Peanut Butter Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the Peanut Butter Cookie Crust and set aside.
2. Combine chocolate chips, 1 tbsp light corn syrup, and 1 tbsp water in the top of a double boiler. Cook over simmering water until chocolate melts and mixture is smooth. Remove from heat and cool well.
3. Meanwhile, combine peanut butter, 1/3 cup sugar, 3 tbsp light corn syrup, and 3 tbsp water in a 2-qt saucepan. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until sugar dissolves and mixture is well blended. Pour into a bowl and stir in peanuts; cool.
4. Beat egg whites until foamy using an electric mixer at high speed. Gradually add 2 tbsp sugar, 1 tbsp at a time, beating well after each addition. Add vanilla and continue beating until stiff, glossy peaks form when beaters are slowly lifted. Fold egg white mixture into whipped cream.
5. Fold peanut butter mixture into the chocolate mixture. Pour half of peanut butter mixture over cookie crust. Drizzle half of chocolate mixture over filling. Top with remaining filling. Drizzle remaining chocolate in parallel lines over filling.
6. Freeze until firm. Wrap securely in aluminum foil. Return to freezer and continue freezing 8 hours or overnight.
7. Remove from freezer 10 minutes before serving.
8. Peanut Butter Cookie Crust: Crush cookies and melt butter or margarine. Combine them and mix well. Press crumb mixture into bottom and up sides of a 9-inch pie plate. Chill in refrigerator until set.
